title: Ensure no VPC security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0  to port 3389

input_row.title: Ensure no VPC security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0  to port 3389
input_row.description: VPC security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Virtual Server Instances. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 3389.
input_row.rationale: Removing uncontrolled connectivity to remote console services, such as RDP, reduces a server's exposure to risk.
input_row.audit: Perform the following tasks to determine if the account is configured as prescribed: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->Security Groups. 3. For each security group, perform the following: a. Select the security group name. b. Ensure no Inbound Rule exists that has a port range that includes port 3389 and has a Source of 0.0.0.0/0. Note that a port range value of ALL or a port range that includes port 3389, e.g. 3300-3400, are inclusive of port 3389.
input_row.remediation: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->Security Groups. 3. For each security group, perform the following: a. Select the access control list name. b. Identify the Inbound rule to be removed. c. Using the Options icon, select Delete.
input_row.impact: For updating an existing environment, care should be taken to ensure that administrators currently relying on an ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 have access to port 3389 through another, more restrictive, access control list.
input_row.default_value: There are no default rules in the default security group with a value of 3389.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_vpc_security_group_ingress_rules_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would perform the following checks:

1. **API Calls / CLI Methods:**
   - Use the IBM Cloud CLI command: `ibmcloud target --region <region>` to set the target region for the VPC resources.
   - Use the command: `ibmcloud schematics workspace get -w <workspace-name> -i <resource-group>` to get details of the VPC resources in the specified workspace.

2. **Specific Resource Enumeration:**
   - Enumerate all security groups within the identified VPC by executing: `ibmcloud iam security-groups` to retrieve all security groups.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - For each security group, check the inbound rules using the command: `ibmcloud network security-groups get <security-group-id>`.
   - Inspect the inbound rules specifically looking for:
     - Rules with `Source` set to `0.0.0.0/0`.
     - Rules allowing access to `port 3389` or any range that includes it, such as `3300-3400`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - The check will pass if no security group has inbound rules allowing access from `0.0.0.0/0` to `port 3389`, or any ranges that include it.
   - It will fail if any security group is found with such a rule.

5. **Example:**
   - A successful check will output no relevant inbound rules for `0.0.0.0/0` to `port 3389`.
   - If such a rule is detected, the program should log that rule and indicate non-compliance regarding the security best practice.
